  • 2. Component-Level Data Types

2. Component-Level Data Types

Attribution

class Attribution(contributor=None, modified=None, changeMessage=None, creator=None, created=None)

Bases: object

Attribution Information for a Genealogy, Conclusion, Subject and child classes

Parameters:

  • contributor (Agent, optional) – Contributor to object being attributed.

  • modified (timestamp, optional) – timestamp for when this record was modified.

  • changeMessage (str, optional) – Birth date (YYYY-MM-DD).

  • creator (Agent, optional) – Creator of object being attributed.

  • created (timestamp, optional) – timestamp for when this record was created

Raises:

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Attribution'
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'

Conclusion (BaseType)

class Conclusion(id=None, lang=None, sources=None, analysis=None, notes=None, confidence=None, attribution=None)

Bases: object

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

Parameters:

  • id (str, optional) – A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

  • lang (str, optional) – The language code of the conclusion.

  • sources (list[SourceReference], optional) – A list of source references that support the conclusion.

  • analysis (Document | Resource, optional) – A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

  • notes (list[Note], optional) – A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

  • confidence (ConfidenceLevel, optional) – The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

  • attribution (Attribution, optional) – Information about who contributed the conclusion and when.

  • uri (Resource, optional) – A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

  • links (_LinkList, optional) – A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

add_note(note)
Parameters:

note (Note)

add_source_reference(source_to_add)
Parameters:

source_to_add (SourceReference)

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Conclusion'
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'

Bases: Qualifier

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • value (str | None)

High = 'http://gedcomx.org/High'
Low = 'http://gedcomx.org/Low'
Medium = 'http://gedcomx.org/Medium'
property description

class IdentifierType(arg, /, *, by='auto')

Bases: ExtensibleEnum

Parameters:

  • arg (Any)

  • by (Literal['auto', 'name', 'value'])

Return type:

_EnumItem

Authority = IdentifierType.Authority
ChildAndParentsRelationship = IdentifierType.ChildAndParentsRelationship
Deprecated = IdentifierType.Deprecated
External = IdentifierType.External
FamilySearchId = IdentifierType.FamilySearchId
Other = IdentifierType.Other
Persistent = IdentifierType.Persistent
Primary = IdentifierType.Primary
make_uid(length=10, alphabet='abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789')

Generate a cryptographically secure alphanumeric UID.

Parameters:

  • length (int) – Number of characters to generate (must be > 0).

  • alphabet (str) – Characters to choose from (default: A-Za-z0-9).

Returns:

A random string of length characters from alphabet.

Return type:

str

Note

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • value (str | None)

  • lang (str | None)

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/TextValue'
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'

Address

class Address(value=None, city=None, country=None, postalCode=None, stateOrProvince=None, street=None, street2=None, street3=None, street4=None, street5=None, street6=None)

Bases: object

A GedcomX Address Data Type A GedcomX Address Data Type.

Represents a postal address according to the GedcomX conceptual model.

Parameters:

  • value (str, optional) – A complete address as a single string.

  • city (str, optional) – Name of the city or town.

  • country (str, optional) – Name of the country.

  • postalCode (str, optional) – Postal or ZIP code.

  • stateOrProvince (str, optional) – Name of the state, province, or region.

  • street (str, optional) – First street address line.

  • street2 (str, optional) – Second street address line.

  • street3 (str, optional) – Third street address line.

  • street4 (str, optional) – Fourth street address line.

  • street5 (str, optional) – Fifth street address line.

  • street6 (str, optional) – Sixth street address line.

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Address'
property value: str
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'

Subject

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

param id:

A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

type id:

str, optional

param lang:

The language code of the conclusion.

type lang:

str, optional

param sources:

A list of source references that support the conclusion.

type sources:

list[SourceReference], optional

param analysis:

A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

type analysis:

Document | Resource, optional

param notes:

A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

type notes:

list[Note], optional

param confidence:

The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

type confidence:

ConfidenceLevel, optional

param attribution:

Information about who contributed the conclusion and when.

type attribution:

Attribution, optional

param uri:

A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

type uri:

Resource, optional

param links:

A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

type links:

_LinkList, optional

Gender

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

param id:

A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

type id:

str, optional

param lang:

The language code of the conclusion.

type lang:

str, optional

param sources:

A list of source references that support the conclusion.

type sources:

list[SourceReference], optional

param analysis:

A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

type analysis:

Document | Resource, optional

param notes:

A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

type notes:

list[Note], optional

param confidence:

The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

type confidence:

ConfidenceLevel, optional

param attribution:

Information about who contributed the conclusion and when.

type attribution:

Attribution, optional

param uri:

A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

type uri:

Resource, optional

param links:

A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

type links:

_LinkList, optional

Name

Defines a name of a person.

A Name is intended to represent a single variant of a person’s name. This means that nicknames, spelling variations, or other names (often distinguishable by a name type) should be modeled with separate instances of Name.

Advanced details

The name forms of a name contain alternate representations of the name. A Name MUST contain at least one name form, presumably a representation of the name that is considered proper and well formed in the person’s native, historical cultural context. Other name forms MAY be included, which can be used to represent this name in contexts where the native name form is not easily recognized and interpreted. Alternate forms are more likely in situations where conclusions are being analyzed across cultural context boundaries that have both language and writing script differences.

type

Classification of the name (e.g., BirthName, AlsoKnownAs).

Type:

Optional[NameType]

nameForms

One or more structured representations of the name (full text and parts).

Type:

List[NameForm]

date

Date context for this name (e.g., when the name was used or recorded).

Type:

Optional[Date]

Fact

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

param id:

A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

type id:

str, optional

param lang:

The language code of the conclusion.

type lang:

str, optional

param sources:

A list of source references that support the conclusion.

type sources:

list[SourceReference], optional

param analysis:

A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

type analysis:

Document | Resource, optional

param notes:

A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

type notes:

list[Note], optional

param confidence:

The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

type confidence:

ConfidenceLevel, optional

param attribution:

Information about who contributed the conclusion and when.

type attribution:

Attribution, optional

param uri:

A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

type uri:

Resource, optional

param links:

A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

type links:

_LinkList, optional

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

param id:

A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

type id:

str, optional

param lang:

The language code of the conclusion.

type lang:

str, optional

param sources:

A list of source references that support the conclusion.

type sources:

list[SourceReference], optional

param analysis:

A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

type analysis:

Document | Resource, optional

param notes:

A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

type notes:

list[Note], optional

param confidence:

The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

type confidence:

param attribution:

type attribution:

Attribution, optional

param uri:

A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

type uri:

Resource, optional

param links:

A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

type links:

_LinkList, optional

Date

PlaceReference

defines a reference to a PlaceDescription.

original

The unnormalized, user- or source-provided place text. Keep punctuation and ordering exactly as recorded in the source.

Type:

Optional[str]

description

A gedcomx.PlaceDescription Object or pointer to it. (URI/Resource)

Type:

Optional[Resource|PlaceDescription]

Qualifier

defines the data structure used to supply additional details, annotations, tags, or other qualifying data to a specific data element.

name str

The name of the Qualifier. It is RECOMMENDED that the qualifier name resolve to an element of a constrained vocabulary.

value

The value of the Qualifier. If provided, the name MAY give the semantic meaning of the value.

Type:

Optional[str]

Coverage

GroupRole
